content.problemStatement: Write a program that takes the two different strings as input and prints them in a single line separated by spaces.
constraints: The two strings in a test case are guaranteed to be different
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, two lines follow: the first line is the first string (may contain spaces, leading or trailing), the second line is the second string.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the two input strings separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. The two strings "Code" and "Chef" are read and printed separated by a space.
One test case with strings "Hello" and "World"; they are output on one line with a space between them.
The strings contain leading/trailing spaces. They are preserved, and a single space is inserted between them, resulting in " Foo Bar ".

solution.approach: The task requires no computation beyond reading and printing. For each test case we read the two complete lines (preserving any spaces) and output them with a single space between them. This is O(total number of characters) time and O(1) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) – each character is read and written once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input buffers.

code.Python3:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
if not lines:
    sys.exit()
T = int(lines[0].strip())
idx = 1
out = []
for _ in range(T):
    s1 = lines[idx] if idx < len(lines) else ''
    s2 = lines[idx + 1] if idx + 1 < len(lines) else ''
    out.append(f"{s1} {s2}")
    idx += 2
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
